data.title: BSC ecosystem hot Meme coins generally fell, with PALU down 13.41% in 24 hours.
data.description: According to GMGN market data, Meme coins, a popular cryptocurrency in the BSC ecosystem, have experienced a pullback. PALU is currently trading at $0.0369, down 13.41% in the past 24 hours; Binance Life is trading at $0.22, down 29.87% in the past 24 hours; and 4 is trading at $0.14, down 22.41% in the past 24 hours. Odaily reminds users that Meme coins are subject to significant price fluctuations and advises investors to exercise caution.

data.title: Bitcoin OG whales increase their short positions on BTC and ETH, with total positions exceeding $1.1 billion
data.description: According to Lookonchain, Bitcoin's ancient whales have increased their short positions on BTC and ETH, with total positions exceeding $1.1 billion. They shorted 6,189 BTC at 10x leverage, worth approximately $752.9 million, at a forced liquidation price of $130,810. They shorted 81,203 ETH at 12x leverage, worth approximately $353.1 million, at a forced liquidation price of $4,589.3.

data.title: Morgan Stanley lifts restrictions on wealth clients holding crypto funds
data.description: Morgan Stanley has lifted restrictions on the crypto funds its wealth clients can hold, relying on automated monitoring processes to ensure clients aren't overly concentrated in this volatile asset class. A recently released model by the bank's Global Investment Committee recommends a maximum initial allocation of up to 4% to cryptocurrencies, based on objectives ranging from "wealth preservation" to "opportunistic growth." Currently, advisors are limited to recommending Bitcoin funds from BlackRock and Fidelity, but Morgan Stanley is monitoring industry trends and considering adding other cryptocurrency products. Clients can request investment in any listed cryptocurrency exchange-traded product.

data.title: The number of people worldwide with crypto assets exceeding $1 million has exceeded 240,000, a 40% increase from last year.
data.description: The number of individuals worldwide with cryptocurrency assets worth at least $1 million has reached approximately 241,700, a 40% increase from last year. This number includes 450 cryptocurrency billionaires (those with assets exceeding $100 million) and 36 cryptocurrency billionaires. Research has found that cryptocurrency investors spend approximately 9.7 cents for every $1 in cryptocurrency wealth growth, a marginal propensity to consume more than double that of gains from the stock market or real estate. The additional wealth generated by cryptocurrency gains is expected to contribute $145 billion in additional consumer spending in 2024, representing approximately 0.7% of total U.S. consumption. (CNBC)

data.title: Two addresses are long on SOL today, currently holding a total of $37.8 million in SOL long positions.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi (@ai_9684 xtpa), two addresses went long on SOL this afternoon, currently holding a cumulative long position of $37.8 million USD, ranking them in the top 5 and top 10 of Hyperliquid SOL bullishness rankings, respectively. Address 0x0dd...8a902: Long position of 100,000 SOL with 2x leverage at an opening price of $219.68 USD, plus a long position of $26.12 million USD in ETH. Address 0x8d0...59244: Long position of 70,000 SOL with 20x leverage at an opening price of $221.549 USD, plus a long position of $4.04 million USD in LTC.

data.title: Binance to Launch BNB Smart Chain Trading Competition with Token Rewards
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to introduce a BNB Smart Chain Trading Competition on Binance Alpha. This event will run from 2025-10-10 16:00 (UTC) to 2025-10-24 16:00 (UTC), allowing participants to trade Ark of Panda (AOP), STBL (STBL), and Bless (BLESS) through their Binance Wallet (Keyless) or Binance Alpha. Eligible users can compete for exclusive token rewards during this period.

**Competition Details**

Participants will be ranked based on their cumulative trading volume of AOP, STBL, and BLESS tokens. The top traders will receive rewards according to the specified reward structure. The AOP Trading Volume Pool will distribute 8,000,000 AOP tokens equally among the top 10,000 users, with each receiving 800 AOP. Similarly, the STBL Trading Volume Pool will allocate 2,500,000 STBL tokens, and the BLESS Trading Volume Pool will share 11,000,000 BLESS tokens among the top 10,000 users, with each receiving 250 STBL and 1,100 BLESS, respectively.

**Participation Guidelines**

Only trades executed via Binance Wallet (Keyless) or Binance Alpha will qualify for this competition. Limit orders will receive a fourfold weighting in prize pool calculations, enhancing participants' chances of winning. There are no volume caps, and both purchases and sales during the promotion period count towards the competition. Participants can earn rewards from each trading task pool if they meet the requirements. However, transactions related to token bridging and Binance Alpha-to-Alpha trading volume are excluded from the competition.

**Terms and Conditions**

Participants must comply with Binance's terms and conditions, including account verification and holding an active Binance Wallet. Binance reserves the right to disqualify users involved in dishonest behavior or those who tamper with the program code. The platform may amend the competition terms at its discretion without prior notice.

data.title: U.S. October One-Year Inflation Rate Expected at 4.6%
data.description: According to PANews, the initial estimate for the United States' one-year inflation rate in October is projected to be 4.6%. This figure is slightly below the anticipated 4.7% and matches the previous value of 4.70%.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation: Will support Tornado Cash developers through new funding mechanism
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation announced its support for privacy through a new funding mechanism. The Ethereum Funding Coordination Team, in conjunction with the Keyring Network, has launched an initiative to introduce a new method for influencing funding within the Ethereum ecosystem. The zkVerified permissioned vault on the Keyring mainnet provides access to a compliant and privacy-preserving DeFi lending market. During the first two months, all protocol fees from the Keyring zkVerified vault will be donated to the legal defense fund of Roman Storm and Alexey Pertsev, two Tornado Cash developers currently on trial for releasing open source code. This model ensures that the vault's initial users can directly support the legal defense of privacy-focused developers.

data.title: Binance Launches Zero Fee Promotion for BTC/MXN Trading Pair
data.description: Binance is launching a new promotion with zero transaction fees for the BTC/MXN spot trading pair. The promotion runs from 08:00 on October 13, 2025, to 07:59 on November 13, 2025 (GMT+8).

data.title: Monero Releases 'Fluorine Fermi' Upgrade to Enhance Protection Against Spy Nodes
data.description: The privacy-focused Monero blockchain has released a major update, CLI v 0.18.4.3 "Fluorine Fermi," to enhance protection against spy nodes. In the Monero community, the term "spy node" refers to malicious nodes, groups of nodes, or botnets that can link IP addresses to transaction information, effectively undermining the blockchain's privacy guarantees. The "Fermi" update addresses this issue by implementing an improved node selection algorithm that reduces the likelihood of users connecting to multiple nodes within the same IP subnet, a common tactic used by spy nodes. This prevents users from connecting to large clusters of suspicious IP addresses, directing them to more secure nodes. The "Fermi" update also changes how and to whom emails are sent, making it extremely difficult for spies to track their delivery. This helps Monero's email service avoid these suspicious senders. (Cryptopolitan)

data.title: EU Confirms Adequacy of Cryptocurrency Regulations Amid Stablecoin Concerns
data.description:
According to PANews, the European Commission announced on Friday that existing European cryptocurrency regulations are sufficient to address the risks associated with stablecoins, following calls from the European Central Bank (ECB) for additional safeguards. Despite the introduction of landmark cryptocurrency-specific legislation, Brussels lawmakers face pressure from the ECB to prevent the 'multi-location issuance' model of stablecoins.

The main point of contention is whether multinational stablecoin companies can consider tokens issued within the EU as interchangeable with those held outside the EU. On Tuesday, six cryptocurrency industry associations, including Circle, sent a letter to the EU Commission urging the issuance of guidance to confirm the multi-location issuance model and clarify its operation under the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA).

A spokesperson for the European Commission stated that MiCA provides a robust and balanced framework to address stablecoin risks and is working swiftly to provide clarification. The European Systemic Risk Board has identified inherent risks in the multi-location issuance structure, while the ECB is concerned about potential reserve runs. Stablecoin issuers, however, assert that they have sufficient reserves to handle redemptions.

data.title: Gate Fun launches Chinese MEME competition, with over 792 entries today
data.description: According to official news, Gate Fun has officially launched the Chinese MEME Creation and Trading Competition. During the event, all newly launched tokens that meet the theme requirements are welcome to register and compete for a total of 3,000 GT. Official data indicates that over 792 projects participated in the launch today, and users can directly trade on and off the Gate Alpha platform. High-performing projects will have the opportunity to be listed on the Gate spot market and included in the GateLayer ecosystem's key support list. It is reported that the Chinese MEME token "Sesame Open Door" previously launched on Gate Fun has a market capitalization of over $1.95 million. Gate Fun is the Gate ecosystem's first launch platform specifically designed for MEME tokens. Focusing on "zero-code coin issuance + full-scenario trading," it aims to create a complete ecosystem path from token issuance to circulation for Web 3 creators and investors. It supports one-click coin issuance, making it easy for anyone to start a MEME project.

data.title: Ansem: This round of 0 to 1 innovation is to launch a 24/7 trading market for "all assets"
data.description: Crypto influencer Ansem wrote on the X platform: "This wave of crypto innovation, from 0 to 1, is about creating a 24/7 trading market for "all assets" (not just cryptocurrencies). The launch of Perp Dex will push on-chain speculation to new heights. There is a large amount of sticky capital in the crypto space that is reluctant to exit off-chain. At the same time, with the advent of stablecoin regulation and government support, a large amount of capital is also pouring into the chain. The investment opportunity for the next decade lies in: going long on faster, more efficient, permissionless global financial protocols, while shorting the lagging vested interests in traditional finance."

data.title: Two Prime: Bitcoin-backed loans reached $827 million in the third quarter
data.description: Two Prime announced that its Bitcoin finance subsidiary, Two Prime Lending Limited, issued $827 million in Bitcoin-backed loans in the third quarter, bringing its total Bitcoin-backed loans to $2.55 billion to date. Its clients include Nasdaq-listed mining companies CleanSpark and Hut 8, as well as Kindly MD and Flowdesk. (Businesswire)

data.title: Aster: The launch of the Stage 2 airdrop checker will be delayed
data.description: Aster, the perpetual contract DEX, announced on the X platform that the launch of the Stage 2 airdrop checker will be delayed. The team is actively working on resolving this issue and will push for the airdrop checker to be launched as soon as possible.

data.title: Major Banks Collaborate on Joint Stablecoin Initiative
data.description: According to BlockBeats, several leading global banks are collaborating on a joint stablecoin project. The initiative includes Santander, Bank of America, Barclays, BNP Paribas, Citi, Deutsche Bank, Goldman Sachs, MUFG, TD Bank, and UBS. This collaboration aims to explore the potential of stablecoins in the financial sector, reflecting a growing interest among major financial institutions in digital currencies.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Opens with Modest Gains
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. stock market opened with slight increases across the major indices. The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose by 0.28%, the S&P 500 increased by 0.11%, and the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.07%.

data.title: The Macau Monetary Authority plans to complete the research and development of the digital Macau head by the end of the year and support on-demand exchange by individuals
data.description: The Macau Monetary Authority stated that a prototype system for the "Digital Pataca" has been built and the first phase of research and development is expected to be completed by the end of this year. Analysts pointed out that the integration of the Digital Pataca platform with the mainland platform is inevitable, and individuals can decide whether to exchange cash or deposits for digital currency (digital RMB or Macau Pataca) according to their needs. (Macau Citizen Daily)

data.title: A whale liquidated his GIGGLE position 5 hours ago and made a profit of 200 U
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi's monitoring, a whale who had a heavy position in Chinese Meme at a high point liquidated GIGGLE 5 hours ago, making a profit of 200 U. This was the first token sold by the whale among the tokens he had recently built up, and his position was only more than 6,000 US dollars.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Expands Cryptocurrency Investment Options for All Clients
data.description:
According to PANews, Morgan Stanley announced on Friday that it is broadening its cryptocurrency investment options to all clients, allowing investments in any type of account, including retirement accounts. Starting October 15, financial advisors will be able to recommend cryptocurrency funds to any client. Previously, this option was limited to clients with a higher risk tolerance, assets of at least $1.5 million, and those wishing to invest in taxable brokerage accounts.

Insiders revealed that as Morgan Stanley removes qualification requirements for cryptocurrency funds, the company will rely on automated monitoring processes to ensure clients do not overly concentrate their investments in this volatile asset class. The bank's Global Investment Committee recently released a model suggesting an initial cryptocurrency allocation of up to 4%, depending on goals ranging from "wealth preservation" to "opportunistic growth." Currently, financial advisors are limited to recommending Bitcoin funds from BlackRock and Fidelity, but Morgan Stanley is monitoring industry trends and considering adding other cryptocurrencies to these products. Additionally, clients can request investments in any listed cryptocurrency exchange-traded products.
